metadata.seed: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

patient.query: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

automatic_thoughts: I'm scared I'm going to get fired
I keep overthinking everything

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling quite anxious at work, and it seems that you might be overthinking things a bit. Let's explore together if there are any specific situations or tasks that make you feel this way.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I do feel really anxious sometimes, especially when I have to present in front of my team. It just feels like everyone’s judging my every word, and then I start thinking about all the ways it could go wrong. It’s like my mind just spirals, and I can’t focus on what I’m supposed to be doing.

triggers: presenting in front of my team
automatic_thoughts: everyone’s judging my every word
all the ways it could go wrong
my mind just spirals
behaviors: can’t focus on what I’m supposed to be doing

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're dealing with some anxiety when presenting to your team. It's common for people to feel that way, especially when they fear being judged. Let's explore together if there are any specific situations or thoughts that might be contributing to this feeling.

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely feels like that fear of judgment is always there. I keep thinking about how I messed up last time and how people probably still remember it. Sometimes I even imagine them whispering about me. It’s just exhausting, you know? And then I freeze up when it’s my turn to speak.

automatic_thoughts: I messed up last time
people probably still remember it
they are whispering about me
behaviors: freeze up when it's my turn to speak

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Fear of speaking to people in authority (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden of fear and self-doubt. Let's explore together why you might be so concerned about making mistakes or saying the wrong thing in front of others. Perhaps we can find ways to lessen this anxiety and help you feel more confident when it's your turn to speak.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just…it feels so embarrassing to mess up. Like, if I make a mistake, it confirms all those negative things I think about myself, you know? I worry people will see me as incompetent or something, like I don’t belong there. It’s like I’m always fighting this inner battle, and I’m just so tired of it.

automatic_thoughts: it confirms all those negative things I think about myself
people will see me as incompetent
I don’t belong there

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand that you're feeling quite overwhelmed and embarrassed when you make a mistake. It seems like these feelings might be linked to some deeper beliefs about yourself, such as concerns about competence or belonging. Let's explore these thoughts together and see if we can find a more balanced perspective.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess there's a part of me that really believes I need to be perfect to fit in. It’s hard to shake off that feeling. And when I mess up, it just feels like proof that I’m not good enough. I don’t want to feel that way, but it’s like I’m stuck in this loop.

automatic_thoughts: I need to be perfect to fit in
When I mess up, it feels like proof that I’m not good enough

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're holding yourself to a very high standard, and it can be challenging when things don't go as planned. It's important to remember that everyone makes mistakes, and they don't define our worth. Let's explore together if there are any other thoughts or beliefs contributing to this pressure you feel.

patient.query: Yeah, I get that everyone messes up, but it’s like I can’t help but focus on my own mistakes. I keep thinking if I just try harder or prepare more, maybe I won’t feel this way. Sometimes I feel so much pressure to prove myself, but then it just makes everything feel heavier. It’s confusing.

automatic_thoughts: I can't help but focus on my own mistakes
If I just try harder or prepare more, maybe I won't feel this way
I feel so much pressure to prove myself

llm_response.response: It's understandable to feel anxious when you're focusing on your own mistakes, especially if you're putting pressure on yourself to perform well. Let's explore together if there are other ways to approach this situation that might help reduce some of the anxiety and pressure you're feeling.

patient.query: I guess I see what you’re saying, but it’s hard to imagine a different way. It feels like if I let go of that pressure, I might just fall apart or really mess things up. I want to feel less anxious, but at the same time, a part of me thinks I need that pressure to do well. It’s just so conflicting.

automatic_thoughts: if I let go of that pressure, I might just fall apart
I need that pressure to do well

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're dealing with a lot of pressure and it's causing some anxiety. Let's explore together if there might be other ways to approach this situation that could help reduce your stress while still allowing you to perform well.

patient.query: I mean, I know you’re trying to help, and I appreciate it. But thinking about other ways kind of makes me feel uneasy. Like, what if I try something different and it doesn’t work? It feels safer to stick with what I know, even if it’s stressful. It’s like I’m caught between wanting to change and being scared to.

automatic_thoughts: what if it doesn’t work?
it feels safer to stick with what I know

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand that you're feeling a bit uneasy about exploring new options. It's natural to have concerns, especially when we're stepping out of our comfort zone. Let's talk more about what might be causing this discomfort and see if we can find ways to manage it together.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just this nagging fear of the unknown. I worry about what could happen if I try something new and it backfires. Part of me wants to break free from this anxiety, but the other part keeps saying that staying in my bubble is safer, even if it’s not the best. It feels like I’m always weighing the risks and it just gets so overwhelming.

automatic_thoughts: what could happen if I try something new and it backfires
staying in my bubble is safer

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're facing a challenging situation, and it's natural to feel anxious when trying something new. Let's explore together how we can approach this fear and find ways to move forward that feel more comfortable for you.

patient.query: I guess I just don’t want to keep feeling stuck forever. It’s frustrating, but then again, the thought of opening up to new ways feels so daunting. I want to believe there’s a way out that doesn’t feel so scary, but I’m just not sure how to get there. It’s like I’m standing at the edge of a cliff and can’t decide whether to jump or

triggers: thought of opening up to new ways
automatic_thoughts: there’s a way out that doesn’t feel so scary
I’m just not sure how to get there
behaviors: standing at the edge of a cliff

neutral: Unable to find way around a familiar environment (finding)
contradiction: Able to find way around a familiar environment (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're feeling stuck and unsure about making a change. Change can indeed feel daunting, but it's also an opportunity for growth. Let's explore together if there might be smaller steps we could take to help you move forward without feeling overwhelmed.
